attributes.title: Exercises — Log pipeline (Kibana)
attributes.description: Ingest health for Filebeat → Logstash → Elasticsearch
attributes.panelsJSON:
[{"version":"8.15.5","type":"search","gridData":{"x":0,"y":0,"w":24,"h":15,"i":"1"},"panelIndex":"1","embeddableConfig":{},"panelRefName":"panel_1"},{"version":"8.15.5","type":"search","gridData":{"x":24,"y":0,"w":24,"h":15,"i":"2"},"panelIndex":"2","embeddableConfig":{},"panelRefName":"panel_2"},{"version":"8.15.5","type":"markdown","gridData":{"x":0,"y":15,"w":48,"h":8,"i":"3"},"panelIndex":"3","embeddableConfig":{"attributes":{"title":"Recovering missed logs","markdown":"### If Grafana alerted or ingest flatlined

1. Note the **alert start time** (Grafana → Alerting → Alert history).
2. On the host, inspect JSON log files for that window:
   - `apps/java/logs/demo-app.json.log`
   - `apps/python/logs/demo-app.json.log`
   - `apps/rust/logs/demo-app.json.log`
   - `apps/react-node/logs/demo-app.json.log`
   - `apps/postgres/logs/postgresql-*`
3. Compare with this dashboard: lines on disk but absent here were **not shipped** during the outage.
4. Container logs: `podman compose -f docker-compose.observability.yml logs filebeat logstash --tail 100`.

After recovery, Filebeat resumes from its registry and may ship backlog."}},"title":"Recovering missed logs"}]
